channel.title: TaskFlow on FsFlow
channel.description: Recent content in TaskFlow on FsFlow

item.title: TaskFlow.apply
item.description:
Applies a task-flow-wrapped function to a task-flow-wrapped value.

let apply (flow: TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value -> 'next>) (value: TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'next>

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.bind
item.description:
Sequences a task-flow-producing continuation after a successful value.

let bind (binder: 'value -> TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'next>) (flow: TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'next>

Remarks

This is the “flatmap” operation for TaskFlow. It allows for dependent asynchronous steps where the second flow depends on the value produced by the first.

Parameters

  • binder: A function of type 'value -> TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'next>.
  • flow: The source task flow.

Returns

A new task flow representing the combined workflow.

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.catch
item.description:
Catches exceptions raised during execution and maps them to a typed error.

let catch (handler: exn -> 'error) (flow: TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Parameters

  • handler: A function of type exn -> 'error to map the exception.
  • flow: The source task flow.

Returns

A task flow that converts exceptions into success-path errors.

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.delay
item.description:
Defers task flow construction until execution time.

let delay (factory: unit -> TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Parameters

  • factory: A function of type unit -> TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>.

Returns

A task flow that evaluates the factory only when executed.

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.env
item.description:
let env<'env, 'error> : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'env>

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.error
item.description:
Creates a failing task flow.

let error (failure: 'error) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Parameters

  • error: The failure value of type 'error.

Returns

A TaskFlow that always fails.

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.fail
item.description:
Alias for error that reads well in some call sites.

let fail (failure: 'error) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.fromAsyncFlow
item.description:
let fromAsyncFlow (flow: AsyncFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.fromFlow
item.description:
let fromFlow (flow: Flow<'env, 'error, 'value>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.fromOption
item.description:
Lifts an option into a task flow with the supplied error.

let fromOption (error: 'error) (value: 'value option) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Parameters

  • error: The error to return if the option is None.
  • value: The option to lift.

Returns

A task flow succeeding with the option’s value or failing.

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.fromResult
item.description:
Lifts a standard Result into a task flow.

let fromResult (result: Result<'value, 'error>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Parameters

  • result: The result to lift.

Returns

A task flow mirroring the result.

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.fromTask
item.description:
let fromTask (coldTask: ColdTask<'value>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.fromTaskResult
item.description:
let fromTaskResult (coldTask: ColdTask<Result<'value, 'error>>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.fromValueOption
item.description:
Lifts a value option into a task flow with the supplied error.

let fromValueOption (error: 'error) (value: 'value voption) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Parameters

  • error: The error of type 'error to return if the value option is ValueNone.
  • value: The value option to lift.

Returns

A task flow succeeding with the option’s value or failing.

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.ignore
item.description:
Maps the successful value of a task flow to unit.

let ignore (flow: TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, unit>

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.localEnv
item.description:
Transforms the environment before running a task flow.

let localEnv (mapping: 'outerEnvironment -> 'innerEnvironment) (flow: TaskFlow<'innerEnvironment, 'error, 'value>) : TaskFlow<'outerEnvironment, 'error, 'value>

Parameters

  • mapping: A function of type 'outerEnvironment -> 'innerEnvironment.
  • flow: The task flow expecting 'innerEnvironment.

Returns

A task flow that accepts 'outerEnvironment.

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.map
item.description:
Maps the successful value of a task flow.

let map (mapper: 'value -> 'next) (flow: TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'next>

Parameters

  • mapper: A function of type 'value -> 'next to transform the success value.
  • flow: The source task flow of type TaskFlow.

Returns

A new TaskFlow with the transformed success value.

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.map2
item.description:
Combines two task flows with a mapping function.

let map2 (mapper: 'left -> 'right -> 'value) (left: TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'left>) (right: TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'right>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Parameters

  • mapper: A function of type 'left -> 'right -> 'value.
  • left: The first task flow.
  • right: The second task flow.

Returns

A task flow with the combined value.

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.map3
item.description:
Combines three task flows with a mapping function.

let map3 (mapper: 'left -> 'middle -> 'right -> 'value) (left: TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'left>) (middle: TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'middle>) (right: TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'right>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.mapError
item.description:
Maps the error value of a task flow.

let mapError (mapper: 'error -> 'nextError) (flow: TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'nextError, 'value>

Parameters

  • mapper: A function of type 'error -> 'nextError.
  • flow: The source task flow.

Returns

A new TaskFlow with the transformed error type.

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.ok
item.description:
Creates a successful task flow.

let ok (value: 'value) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Parameters

  • value: The success value of type 'value.

Returns

A TaskFlow that always succeeds.

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.orElse
item.description:
Falls back to another task flow when the source flow fails.

let orElse (fallback: TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>) (flow: TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.orElseAsync
item.description:
Turns a pure validation result into a task flow with task-provided failure.

let orElseAsync (errorAsync: Async<'error>) (result: Result<'value, unit>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Returns

A TaskFlow that mirrors the result or produces the task error.

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.orElseAsyncFlow
item.description:
let orElseAsyncFlow (errorFlow: AsyncFlow<'env, 'error, 'error>) (result: Result<'value, unit>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.orElseFlow
item.description:
let orElseFlow (errorFlow: Flow<'env, 'error, 'error>) (result: Result<'value, unit>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.orElseTask
item.description:
let orElseTask (errorTask: Task<'error>) (result: Result<'value, unit>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.orElseTaskFlow
item.description:
let orElseTaskFlow (errorFlow: TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'error>) (result: Result<'value, unit>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.orElseWith
item.description:
Falls back to another task flow when the source flow fails.

let orElseWith (fallback: 'error -> TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>) (flow: TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Parameters

  • fallback: The fallback flow of type TaskFlow.
  • flow: The primary task flow.

Returns

A task flow that tries the primary first, then the fallback.

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.provideLayer
item.description:
Provides a derived environment from a layer flow to a downstream task flow.

let provideLayer (layer: TaskFlow<'input, 'error, 'environment>) (flow: TaskFlow<'environment, 'error, 'value>) : TaskFlow<'input, 'error, 'value>

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.read
item.description:
let read (projection: 'env -> 'value) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.readEnvironment
item.description:
Reads the application environment half of a runtime-context environment.

let readEnvironment (projection: 'env -> 'value) : TaskFlow<RuntimeContext<'runtime, 'env>, 'error, 'value>

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.readRuntime
item.description:
Reads the runtime half of a runtime-context environment.

let readRuntime (projection: 'runtime -> 'value) : TaskFlow<RuntimeContext<'runtime, 'env>, 'error, 'value>

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.run
item.description:
Executes a task flow with the provided environment and cancellation token.

let run (environment: 'env) (cancellationToken: CancellationToken) (TaskFlow operation: TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>) : Task<Result<'value, 'error>>

Parameters

  • environment: The environment of type 'env.
  • cancellationToken: The CancellationToken.
  • flow: The TaskFlow to execute.

Returns

A Task containing the Result.

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.runContext
item.description:
Runs a task flow against a RuntimeContext and its internal cancellation token.

let runContext (context: RuntimeContext<'runtime, 'env>) (flow: TaskFlow<RuntimeContext<'runtime, 'env>, 'error, 'value>) : Task<Result<'value, 'error>>

Parameters

  • context: The RuntimeContext providing services and cancellation.
  • flow: The task flow to run.

Returns

A Task with the final result.

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.sequence
item.description:
Transforms a sequence of task flows into a task flow of a sequence and stops at the first failure.

let sequence (flows: seq<TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value list>

Parameters

  • flows: A sequence of task flows.

Returns

A task flow containing the list of successful results.

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.succeed
item.description:
Alias for ok that reads well in some call sites.

let succeed (value: 'value) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.tap
item.description:
Runs a task-based side effect on success and preserves the original value.

let tap (binder: 'value -> TaskFlow<'env, 'error, unit>) (flow: TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Parameters

  • binder: A function that produces a side-effect task flow from the successful value.
  • flow: The source task flow.

Returns

A task flow that preserves the original success value after the side effect.

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.tapError
item.description:
Runs a task-based side effect on failure and preserves the original error.

let tapError (binder: 'error -> TaskFlow<'env, 'error, unit>) (flow: TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Parameters

  • binder: A function that produces a side-effect task flow from the error value.
  • flow: The source task flow.

Returns

A task flow that preserves the original error after the side effect.

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskBuilders.taskFlow
item.description:
The .NET taskFlow { } computation expression.

Remarks

This builder enables using `let!`, `do!`, and other standard computation expression features with `TaskFlow`. It supports seamless binding to many types: `TaskFlow` (standard flow) `AsyncFlow` (lifts to task-based flow) `Flow` (lifts synchronous to task-based) `Task` and `Task` (auto-wraps in Ok) `ValueTask` and `ValueTask` (auto-wraps in Ok) `Result` (lifts pure result to task-based flow) `FSharpAsync` (auto-wraps in Ok) It also supports `Guard.Of` and `Guard.MapError` for inline check-like sources and existing-error remapping before binding into the flow.

Information

  • Module: TaskBuilders
  • Source: source

Examples

let getUser (id: int) = taskFlow {
    let! db = TaskFlow.read (fun env -> env.Db)
    let! user = db.FindUserAsync(id) |> Guard.Of (UserNotFound id)
    do! Task.Delay(100) // Bind to Task
    return user
}

item.title: TaskFlow Runtime
item.description:
This page shows the source-documented task runtime surface: the runtime context and the task-specific operational helpers.

Runtime context

  • type RuntimeContext: Captures the two-context shape of a task workflow execution: runtime services, application capabilities, and the cancellation token for the current run. source
  • module RuntimeContext: Helpers for building and reshaping RuntimeContext{runtime, env} values. source
  • RuntimeContext.create: Creates a runtime context from the supplied runtime services, environment, and cancellation token. source
  • RuntimeContext.runtime: Reads the runtime half of a runtime context. source
  • RuntimeContext.environment: Reads the application environment half of a runtime context. source
  • RuntimeContext.cancellationToken: Reads the cancellation token stored in a runtime context. source
  • RuntimeContext.mapRuntime: Maps the runtime half of a runtime context. source
  • RuntimeContext.mapEnvironment: Maps the application environment half of a runtime context. source
  • RuntimeContext.withRuntime: Replaces the runtime half of a runtime context. source
  • RuntimeContext.withEnvironment: Replaces the environment half of a runtime context. source

Runtime helpers

  • module TaskFlow.Runtime: Task-native runtime helpers for operational concerns like logging, timeout, retry, and scoped cleanup. source
  • TaskFlow.Runtime.cancellationToken: Reads the current runtime cancellation token. source
  • TaskFlow.Runtime.catchCancellation: Converts an OperationCanceledException into a typed error. source
  • TaskFlow.Runtime.ensureNotCanceled: Returns a typed error immediately when the runtime token is already canceled. source
  • TaskFlow.Runtime.sleep: Suspends the flow for the specified duration while observing cancellation. source
  • TaskFlow.Runtime.log: Writes a fixed log message through the environment-provided logger. source
  • TaskFlow.Runtime.logWith: Writes a log message computed from the current environment. source
  • TaskFlow.Runtime.useWithAcquireRelease: Acquires a resource, uses it, and always runs the release action. source
  • TaskFlow.Runtime.timeout: Fails with the supplied error when the flow does not complete before the timeout. source
  • TaskFlow.Runtime.timeoutToOk: Returns the supplied success value when the flow times out. source
  • TaskFlow.Runtime.timeoutToError: Forwards to timeout for a typed failure on timeout. source
  • TaskFlow.Runtime.timeoutWith: Runs a fallback flow when the original flow times out. source
  • TaskFlow.Runtime.retry: Retries a flow according to the supplied policy. source

item.title: TaskFlow.toTask
item.description:
Converts a task flow into a hot Task.

let toTask (environment: 'env) (cancellationToken: CancellationToken) (flow: TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>) : Task<Result<'value, 'error>>

Remarks

This is an alias for run that emphasizes the conversion to a standard .NET Task.

Parameters

  • environment: The environment of type 'env.
  • cancellationToken: The CancellationToken.
  • flow: The task flow to convert.

Returns

A started task.

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.traverse
item.description:
Transforms a sequence of values into a task flow and stops at the first failure.

let traverse (mapping: 'value -> TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'next>) (values: seq<'value>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'next list>

Parameters

  • mapping: A function of type 'value -> TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'next>.
  • values: The input sequence.

Returns

A task flow containing the list of successful results.

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.value
item.description:
Alias for ok that reads well in some call sites.

let value (item: 'value) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'value>

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source

item.title: TaskFlow.zip
item.description:
Combines two task flows into a tuple of their values.

let zip (left: TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'left>) (right: TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'right>) : TaskFlow<'env, 'error, 'left * 'right>

Parameters

  • left: The first task flow.
  • right: The second task flow.

Returns

A task flow containing a tuple of results.

Information

  • Module: TaskFlow
  • Source: source
